attacking_style: Opportunistic attacker who capitalizes on tactical chaos and echelons in final 10km, or launches powerful solo chases when gaps open. Excels in time-trial efforts and bridging moves rather than pure explosive attacks.
team_tactics: QuickStep/Soudal deploys him as flexible asset who can bridge to threatening moves to maintain numerical advantage, or counter-attack from team-generated echelons. Benefits from team's willingness to spend energy early for positioning.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling cobbled classics and time trials in all conditions. Strong in crosswinds and echelon situations. Paris-Roubaix terrain suits him particularly well when avoiding mechanicals/crashes.
vulnerability: Highly susceptible to crashes and equipment failures on cobbles despite suitability to terrain. Has fallen or crashed in multiple Paris-Roubaix editions (2021, 2022) when positioned for results. Team numerical advantage doesn't always translate to control in sprint finishes.
key_patterns: Multiple Paris-Roubaix podium threats ended by crashes/mechanicals (2021, 2022), suggesting positioning or equipment vulnerability.
Elite time trial capacity in wet conditions, defeating Van Aert at 2022 Tour opener.
Powerful solo chaser: closed 40s to 18s at 2019 Roubaix, won from late attack after echelons.
Used tactically to bridge gaps and preserve team numbers rather than wait for chase.
Thrives in crosswind/echelon chaos, winning from Sky-triggered split at 2017 Vuelta.
form_trajectory: neutral: Strong 2022 TT win followed by 2023 Classic selection but failed team execution suggests maintained physical level without breakthrough results
